# vyre v0.4.0 â€” GPU IR with byte-identical conformance

**Release date:** 2026-04-17
**Commit:** main@<fill-at-tag-time>
**Tag:** v0.4.0

Eight crates released to crates.io in a single coordinated publish:

| Crate | Version | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| [`vyre`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre) | 0.4.0 | IR, ops catalog, lowering, `VyreBackend` trait â€” backend-agnostic |
| [`vyre-spec`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-spec) | 0.1.0 | Frozen data contracts (5-year SemVer): OpSpec, AlgebraicLaw, Category, Invariant |
| [`vyre-reference`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-reference) | 0.1.0 | Pure-Rust CPU reference interpreter â€” byte-identical oracle |
| [`vyre-wgpu`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-wgpu) | 0.1.0 | wgpu backend â€” implements VyreBackend on any wgpu-capable GPU |
| [`vyre-std`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-std) | 0.1.0 | DFA, Aho-Corasick, regex â†’ NFA â†’ DFA composites |
| [`vyre-sigstore`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-sigstore) | 0.1.0 | Keyless certificate signing + verification (standalone from conform) |
| [`vyre-conform`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-conform) | 0.1.0 | Maintainer harness â€” backend certification |
| [`vyre-build-scan`](https://crates.io/crates/vyre-build-scan) | 0.1.0 | build.rs filesystem scanner for trait-impl registries |

## What changed

This is a **clean-room release**. Every boundary redrawn. Pre-v0.4.0 artifacts are not source- or binary-compatible â€” treat this as the start of the supported line.

### Architecture

- **Backend-agnostic core**: `vyre` no longer depends on `wgpu`. Runtime, device, buffer pool, pipeline cache, and shader compilation live in the separate `vyre-wgpu` crate. The next backend (CUDA, Metal, Vulkan-native, WebGPU, â€¦) slots in as a sibling crate without touching `vyre`.
- **Split CPU reference**: the CPU interpreter that `vyre-conform` uses as a byte-identity oracle moved from `vyre` into the separate `vyre-reference` crate. Downstream users consume it directly for small-data CPU paths and property-test doubles.
- **Split sigstore**: certificate signing is standalone, not buried inside conform. Downstream auditors verify certificates without pulling the maintainer harness.
- **Conform is maintainer-only**: `vyre-conform` no longer sits on any runtime path. End-user apps consume `vyre` + `vyre-reference` + a backend.

### Public API

- `vyre` exposes exactly `VyreBackend`, `Program`, `validate`, `Match`, `Error`, `Result`, `BackendError`, `DispatchConfig`, plus `ir`/`ops`/`lower`/`error` modules.
- `vyre-conform` exposes exactly ten items: `certify`, `Certificate`, `Violation`, `VyreBackend`, `Finding`, `EnforceGate`, `Oracle`, `Archetype`, `MutationClass`, `{Category, AlgebraicLaw, OpSpec}` re-export.
- Every public item has a real doc comment. `#![deny(missing_docs)]` at every crate root.

### Correctness

- **Deterministic certificates**: two runs of `certify(&backend, inputs)` with identical inputs produce byte-identical certificates. No `monotonic_sequence`, no `elapsed_ms` embedded in cert fields.
- **Byte-identity gate**: every op in `vyre::ops::discovered` has a CPU reference in `vyre-reference` and a conform spec with KAT vectors + adversarial inputs.
- **Every `Err` / `panic!` / `.expect` message prefixed `Fix:` with actionable guidance.**
- One `unsafe` block in the entire workspace: `wgpu::Device::create_pipeline_cache` in `vyre-wgpu`, scoped `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` with documented safety invariant.

### Tooling

- Every crate publishes with `[package.metadata.docs.rs]` enabled. Full feature docs build on docs.rs.
- CI gates: `cargo-deny`, `cargo-public-api` diff, `cargo-semver-checks`, `cargo-udeps`, `cargo-llvm-cov` (85% line floor), `loom`, `miri`, `-D warnings` strict build.
- `cargo-deny` allowlist: MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-*, ISC, Unicode, Zlib, CC0, MPL-2.0, 0BSD. No openssl; rustls only.

### Removed

- Bytecode VM. No opcode interpreter, no stack machine â€” ever.
- `parity-oracle`, `decode-only`, `hash-only`, `primitive-only` feature flags. One feature set, one product.
- Every `pub use X as Y` alias, every `pub use ::*` glob, every `#[deprecated]` attribute, every `// TODO` / `// FIXME` / `// HACK` in non-test code.

## Installation

```sh
# Runtime consumer (most users)
cargo add vyre vyre-reference vyre-wgpu

# Frozen data contracts
cargo add vyre-spec

# Higher-level composites
cargo add vyre-std

# Maintainer harness (backend implementers, spec authors)
cargo add --dev vyre-conform vyre-sigstore
```

## MSRV

Rust 1.85.

MIT OR Apache-2.0, uniformly across every crate.

## Next

- [`vyre-cuda`](https://github.com/santhsecurity/vyre/issues/42): second backend (CUDA-native).
- [`vyre-metal`](https://github.com/santhsecurity/vyre/issues/43): third backend (Apple Metal).
- Expanded op catalog: graph algorithms, more compression codecs, stream ciphers.
